description_long: This object is a vintage portable projector with a robust, boxy form and a textured blue exterior. It features a prominent black carrying handle on top, making it easy to transport. The front side displays a circular lens opening and a control panel with a large rotary knob and a switch, both surrounded by metallic accents. The rear side includes a circular vent with a grill pattern for cooling, as well as rubberized feet for stability. The casing is reinforced with black trim along the edges, and subtle branding is embossed on one side. The overall design is utilitarian, with visible screws and functional elements that suggest durability and ease of maintenance.
description: A vintage blue portable projector with a black handle, front lens, control panel, and rear ventilation grill, featuring a rugged, utilitarian design.
description_view_0: The front view shows the lens opening, control panel with rotary knob and switch, and the black handle on top.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the circular ventilation grill, rubberized feet, and the handle, with no visible controls or lens.
description_view_2: This side view reveals the black trim, handle attachment, and the side profile of the control panel and vents.
description_view_3: The opposite side displays the embossed branding, handle, and a partial view of the ventilation grill, with minimal controls visible.
attribute_brand_logo: There is an embossed logo on the side of the projector, but the brand name is not fully legible. The logo appears as raised text in the same blue color as the body, located on the lower side panel.
attribute_lens_type: fixed lens; circular, medium-sized, with a recessed design and no visible adjustment rings.
attribute_control_panel_design: The control panel is located on the front, featuring a large rotary knob and a toggle switch, both metallic and robust, with no visible digital interface or touch controls.
attribute_ventilation_pattern: A circular grill vent is present on the rear side, composed of radial slats arranged in a fan-like pattern, providing airflow for cooling.
